faq.title: Google Veo 3.1 FAQ — Veo AI Video Generator
faq.description: Frequently asked questions about Google Veo 3.1 AI video generation.

items.question: What is Veo 3.1?
items.answer: Veo 3.1 is Google DeepMind's latest AI video generation model. It generates cinematic-quality videos from text prompts or reference images, with native audio support, advanced scene composition, and up to 1080p resolution. Veo 3.1 is available in two variants: standard and Fast.

items.question: What is Google Veo 3.1 and how is it different from Veo 3.0?
items.answer: Google Veo 3.1 is an updated version of Veo 3.0 with improved visual quality, better prompt adherence, and enhanced native audio generation. Veo 3.1 Fast offers faster generation at the same resolution for quicker iteration.

items.question: Can I use Google Veo 3.1 for free?
items.answer: Yes — you can try Google Veo 3.1 AI video generator free online with sign-up credits on AI Video Generator Free. No credit card required to start.

items.question: What resolution does Veo 3.1 support?
items.answer: Google Veo 3.1 supports up to 1080p video output with aspect ratio options including 16:9, 9:16, and Auto.

items.question: Does Veo 3.1 support audio?
items.answer: Yes. Veo 3.1 includes native audio generation, producing videos with synchronized sound effects and ambient audio that match the visual content.

items.question: How does Google Veo 3.1 compare to other AI video models?
items.answer: Google Veo 3.1 stands out for its cinematic photorealistic quality and advanced scene composition, powered by Google DeepMind's research. It competes directly with Kling 3.0 and Seedance 2.0 in the top tier of AI video generation.
